job_title: View job: Scientist 2-Spatial Biology and Biophysics
job_description:
• 3+ years of relevant experience in situ hybridization, imaging and spatial techniques, preferably in the Biotechnology industry.
• Strong analytical background Strong organizational skills.
• Strong written and oral communication skills.
• Strong troubleshooting and problem solving skills.
• Experience with the 10x Genomics Xenium platform is a plus.
• Skills in image analysis are a plus.
• PhD in Molecular Biology, Biophysics, Physical Chemistry or related field.
• Proven ability to design novel molecular biology assay schemes, as evidenced by peer-reviewed publications or published patents.

• Execute designs with solid modeling software (PTC Creo / Windchill) to create models, assemblies, detailed drawings, and BOMs for designs which may include plastic, stamped metal, and electrically conductive parts.
• Driving the achievement of assigned targets (quality, time, scope, cost) and raising and addressing risks.

• This role requires an efficient, organized, task-oriented person, capable of interacting and communicating with all levels of customer, factories, and internal departments, both verbally and in writing.
• Excellent oral and written communication skills in English and ability to build commitment within diverse and fragmented environments.
• High School diploma with 3 or more years of experience or Vocational Education in engineering or related field.
• Marine Industry service or equivalent experience in the diesel engine industry is strongly preferred.
• Candidates must already have work authorization that would permit them to work for ABB in the US Long Term Disability – 60% of pay – Company paid.
